headline: IPv4 vs IPv6
description: IPv4 (1981) uses 32-bit addresses (~4.3 billion). IPv6 (1998) uses 128-bit addresses (340 undecillion). IPv4 exhausted in 2019 (last /8 allocated). IPv6 adoption: ~45% globally (2025). IPv6 eliminates NAT (every device gets public IP) and simplifies header format.
